mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 150k signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136133k as of 2013-08-01 shows ongoing recovery momentum
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above its 12-month trend, real rates compress and equity risk premiums decline, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors rotate into equities as inflation hedges.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% and CPIAUCSL at 233.318 as of 2013-08-01
regime_hint: zero-bound monetary policy with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between real rate compression and forward returns becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining more than 10% month-over-month signals deteriorating demand-side momentum and forward-looking recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity markets reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 896k as of 2013-07-01 shows housing recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where HOUST declines exceed 10%, or the average forward return in triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises above 7.0%, signaling stagflationary pressure and supply-side constraints that compress profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.9462 and UNRATE at 7.3% as of 2013-08-01
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure with labor market slack
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of INDPRO deceleration and UNRATE elevation shows no significant interaction term (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.8% month-over-month, indicating robust consumer spending and demand-side strength that translates into higher corporate revenues and equity valuations over the subsequent 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 426563M as of 2013-08-01 reflects consumer demand recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of months where RSAFS growth exceeds 0.8%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month rolling window while payroll growth (PAYEMS) simultaneously decelerates below 50k, signaling labor market deterioration that triggers equity sell-offs over the next 5 trading days as recession risk reprices.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month rise > 0.3pp AND PAYEMS monthly growth < 50k
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.3% and PAYEMS at 136133k as of 2013-08-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between UNRATE acceleration and PAYEMS deceleration is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized while the federal funds rate remains below 0.50%, the Fed faces implicit pressure to tighten, but equity markets initially rally on growth optimism over 20 trading days before repricing tightening risk.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 15681.0B (2017$) and FEDFUNDS at 0.08% as of 2013-08-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between growth acceleration and forward returns becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth remains above 1.0% month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) stays below 2.5% annualized, signaling healthy supply-side expansion without inflationary pressure, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as profit margins expand.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly growth > 1.0% AND CPIAUCSL 12-month growth < 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.9462 and CPIAUCSL at 233.318 as of 2013-08-01
regime_hint: goldilocks supply-side expansion with price stability
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of INDPRO growth and inflation moderation shows no significant interaction term (p > 0.10).
